reviewer: weekly-hardening-routine (headless Chromium 141 via CDP; no chrome-devtools-mcp in cloud env)

spec_match.rationale: Uses the explainer's exact element set (menubar/submenu/menulist/menuitem, hr separators, disabled item) and probes the exact interfaces (HTMLMenuBarElement etc.).

interactive.rationale: Probe button + activation readout verified in both configurations: unflagged (HTMLUnknownElement, labelled inert) and flagged (HTMLMenuItemElement, activation works).

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card and page agree on scope.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers unflagged/flagged and disabled items, but arrow-key traversal and nested submenu flyout behaviour were not exercised beyond click activation in the flagged run.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus + HTML PR + Open UI explainer linked.

conformance.rationale: New suite checks the exact upgrade contracts; fails cleanly unflagged, passes behind --enable-blink-features=MenuElements.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Dual-config CDP runs with screenshots read for both; flag banner text matches observed behaviour (experimental-web-platform-features alone did NOT enable it).

telemetry_health.rationale: Upgrade-probe assert wired; KV not queried.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables; menu markup styled readably in both states.

accessibility.rationale: Native roles come with the upgraded elements and the inert state is announced via role=status, but the flagged run did not capture accessibility-tree output for the menubar role mapping.

summary: Element upgrade honestly probed in both configurations; flagged run shows real menubar.

openQuestions.title: Exercise the APG keyboard model behind the flag
openQuestions.detail: In a flagged Chrome/Canary session, tab into the menubar and verify arrow-key traversal, Enter/Escape and submenu flyout open/close, capturing the accessibility tree (menubar/menu/menuitem roles) as evidence.
